nuggets.headline: Thom Yorke once said working without Radiohead was like 'taking off the big flag'.
nuggets.text: Even with immense success, frontman Thom Yorke found a different kind of freedom in solo work. He stated that it was an "odd situation to just sort of start again without the big Radiohead flag, which guarantees this insane level of scrutiny." He found it "nice to take it off," though it also "throws you a bit as well."

source.title: Thom Yorke Is Radiohead's Archetypal Frontman
source.quoteSnippet: 'It’s an odd situation to just sort of start again without the big Radiohead flag, which guarantees this insane level of scrutiny. It’s nice to take it off, but it sort of throws you a bit as well.'

nuggets.imageCaption: Thom Yorke finds different creative freedom outside Radiohead's spotlight.

nuggets.headline: "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi" premiered two years early with an orchestra.
nuggets.text: This track, with its intricate arpeggios, first debuted live on March 27, 2005, at the Ether Festival in London. Thom Yorke and Jonny Greenwood performed it with the Nazareth Orchestra, two years before *In Rainbows* officially came out. The title 'Arpeggi' is simply the Italian plural for 'arpeggio', meaning to play notes from a chord in succession.

source.title: Weird Fishes / Arpeggi by Radiohead - Songfacts
source.quoteSnippet: Thom Yorke and Jonny Greenwood from Radiohead, together with the Nazareth Orchestra, premiered this song at the Ether Festival (Royal Festival Hall, London) on March 27, 2005.

nuggets.headline: If you love this track's intricate mood, try Thom Yorke's other project, Atoms for Peace.
nuggets.text: If the multi-layered, atmospheric beauty and distinctive arpeggios of "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi" are hitting just right, then you absolutely need to check out Atoms for Peace. This band, which Thom Yorke was formerly a member of, shares Radiohead's art rock and electronic leanings, delivering complex rhythms and haunting melodies with a similar cerebral and experimental feel.

source.title: Thom Yorke
source.quoteSnippet: Thom Yorke was formerly a member of Atoms for Peace

nuggets.imageCaption: Atoms for Peace delivers intricate, experimental sounds, similar to Radiohead's 'Weird Fishes'.

artistSummary: Radiohead, an English rock band hailing from Abingdon, Oxfordshire, began as 'On a Friday' before challenging rock conventions and the music industry itself. Consistently releasing critically acclaimed albums like *OK Computer* and *In Rainbows*, they've become known for their cerebral and idiosyncratic approach.
